revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 倭文神社 |画像 = [[画像:Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|270px]]
倭文神社 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]西九条町二丁目14-2 |祭神 = [[天羽槌雄神|武羽槌雄神]]
[[経津主神]]
[[応神天皇|誉田別命]] |社格 = |創建 = [[神護景雲]]2年([[768年]]) |本殿 = 三間社流造{{sfn|奈良県史|p=912}} |例祭 = [[10月10日]] |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 39|緯度秒 = 24.51 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 48|経度秒 = 16.52 |地図 = Nara city }} '''倭文神社'''（しずりじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]西九条町二丁目にある神社。 ==歴史== [[神護景雲]]2年([[768年]])、[[武甕槌命]]が[[鹿島神宮]]より[[春日大社]]に遷座した際、供奉した[[中臣時風]]・[[中臣秀行|秀行]]がこの辰市の地に居住し、[[常陸国]]より祖神[[天羽槌雄神|武羽槌雄神]]（織部の神）を勧請したという{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}現地案内板{{sfn|皇室68号}}。[[倭文氏]]はその後裔で、辰市郷に住み神衣を織ったと伝わる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}現地案内板。古くから、「しずりのやしろ」あるいは「ひずりのやしろ」と呼ばれてきた{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}大和名所図会。 == 祭神 == * [[天羽槌雄神|武羽槌雄神]] * [[経津主神]] * [[応神天皇|誉田別命]] == 社殿 == === 本殿 === 三間社流造、向拝の柱間一間{{sfn|奈良県史|p=912}}。 [[春日大社]]末社三十八所神社の旧本殿の移しと伝わる{{sfn|奈良県史|p=912}}。 === 境内社 === ====若宮社==== 本殿の右に鎮座、祭神は[[仁徳天皇|大鷦鷯命]]{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。 ====蛇塚社==== 境内の東北隅に、独立したような形で鎮座する{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。祭神は[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]で、八阪神社とも呼ばれる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。桁行２尺、梁行１尺６寸の小さな社殿で、少し高くなった所に蛇塚がある{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。 == 祭事 == * 三月祈年祭 * 十月十日例祭{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}} * 十一月新嘗祭 * 毎月一日月次祭 === 蛇祭 === 例祭は'''蛇祭'''として知られる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。麦藁で5メートルほどの大蛇を造り、街を練り歩いたのち境内に納める{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。御供も蛇型で、サトイモの茎で作られる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。子供[[相撲]]が執り行われ、拝殿には[[1890年]]([[明治]]23年)の相撲の[[絵馬]]が掲げられている{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。 [[1915年]]([[大正]]4年)の神社調査書には、当時の祭礼の様子が次のように残されている{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。
「当社例祭ニハ十二ノ人形ヲ作リ饌御供ノ上ニ差シ込ミ別ニ大炬ヲ作リテ神社境内ニ持チ込ミ神饌ノ供進終ルヤ直チニ大炬ニ火ヲ放チ之ヲ焼キ盡スノ式アリ傳説ニ云フ往昔大蛇アリテ人ニ害ヲ為スコト往々アリ李(理)源大師深ク之レヲ憐ミ大蛇ヲ打チ平ケ人々ヲシテ安全ナラシメラレタリト云フ」{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}} == ギャラリー == File:Haiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|拝殿 File:Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|本殿 File:Wakiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|若宮社 File:Chozusha of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|手水舎 File:Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|蛇塚社 File:Honden of Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|蛇塚社 (社殿) File:Otabisho of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|御旅所 File:Otabisho Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|御旅所 (社殿) == 出典 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{commonscat|Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara)}} {{osm box|n|5262213988}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 8 建築|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|title=奈良に残る「春日移し」のお社|date = 2015-秋|publisher = 皇室編集部|journal = 皇室|volume = 68|pages=87|ref={{sfnref|皇室68号}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しすりしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:春日移し|末]]

{{神社 |名称 = 倭文神社 |画像 = [[画像:Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|270px]]
倭文神社 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]西九条町二丁目14-2 |祭神 = [[天羽槌雄神|武羽槌雄神]]
[[経津主神]]
[[応神天皇|誉田別命]] |社格 = 旧村社{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=36}} |創建 = [[神護景雲]]2年([[768年]]) |本殿 = 三間社流造{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=912}} |例祭 = [[10月10日]] |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 39|緯度秒 = 24.51 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 48|経度秒 = 16.52 |地図 = Nara city }} '''倭文神社'''（しずりじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]西九条町二丁目にある神社。 ==歴史== [[神護景雲]]2年([[768年]])、[[武甕槌命]]が[[鹿島神宮]]より[[春日大社]]に遷座した際、供奉した[[中臣時風]]・[[中臣秀行|秀行]]がこの辰市の地に居住し、[[常陸国]]より祖神[[天羽槌雄神|武羽槌雄神]]（織部の神）を勧請したという{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}現地案内板{{sfn|皇室68号}}。[[倭文氏]]はその後裔で、辰市郷に住み神衣を織ったと伝わる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}現地案内板。古くから、「しずりのやしろ」あるいは「ひずりのやしろ」と呼ばれてきた{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}大和名所図会。 == 祭神 == * [[天羽槌雄神|武羽槌雄神]] * [[経津主神]] * [[応神天皇|誉田別命]] == 社殿 == === 本殿 === 三間社流造、向拝の柱間一間{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=912}}。 [[春日大社]]末社三十八所神社の旧本殿の移しと伝わる{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=912}}。 === 境内社 === ====若宮社==== 本殿の右に鎮座、祭神は[[仁徳天皇|大鷦鷯命]]{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。 ====蛇塚社==== 境内の東北隅に、独立したような形で鎮座する{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。祭神は[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]で、八阪神社とも呼ばれる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。桁行２尺、梁行１尺６寸の小さな社殿で、少し高くなった所に蛇塚がある{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。 == 祭事 == * 三月祈年祭 * 十月十日例祭{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}} * 十一月新嘗祭 * 毎月一日月次祭 === 蛇祭 === 例祭は'''蛇祭'''として知られる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。麦藁で5メートルほどの大蛇を造り、街を練り歩いたのち境内に納める{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。御供も蛇型で、サトイモの茎で作られる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。子供[[相撲]]が執り行われ、拝殿には[[1890年]]([[明治]]23年)の相撲の[[絵馬]]が掲げられている{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。 [[1915年]]([[大正]]4年)の神社調査書には、当時の祭礼の様子が次のように残されている{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori). These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神}}'''..."
revision.text:
'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shidori clan|ja|倭文氏}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "Your Name"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto'''.
== Major Shidori Shrines ==
The following shrines are listed in the '''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}''':
* '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)}} (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, {{ill|Isesaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}, [[Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''Shizuka Shrine (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – {{ill|Shizuka Shrine|ja|静神社}} ({{ill|Naka, Ibaraki|ja|那珂市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Koma District, Kai Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)}} (Miyakubo, {{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|ja|韮崎市}}, [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Fuji District, Suruga Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)}} (Hoshiyama, {{ill|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|ja|富士宮市}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Weaving and textiles in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to textile deities]] [[Category:Mythological and legendary Japanese figures]] [[ja:倭文神社]]
[[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] [[Category:倭文神社]][[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]]{{Shizuri Faith}}[[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines forks]]

''Shidori Shrine'' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine ''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto}}'' (also known as ''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto'', ''Ameno Hachio'', or ''Takehatsuchi'') as the ''deity of weaving'', alongside ''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神}}'' (also known as ''Amano Yachichihime'' or ''Amanoorihime''). These shrines were established by the ''{{ill|Shidori clan|ja|倭文氏}}'', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The ''origin of these shrines is attributed to'' the ''{{ill|Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社}}'' in ''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to ''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}'', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of ''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}''. Therefore, these shrines provide ''important historical evidence'' regarding ''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).''
Additionally, in the ''2016 animated film "Your Name"'', the fictional ''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社}}'' is depicted as enshrining ''Shidori deity Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto''.
== Major Shidori Shrines ==
The following shrines are listed in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}'':
* ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province'' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)}} (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, {{ill|Isesaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}, [[Gunma Prefecture]]) * ''Shizuka Shrine (静神社)'' in ''Kuji District, Hitachi Province'' – {{ill|Shizuka Shrine|ja|静神社}} ({{ill|Naka, Ibaraki|ja|那珂市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in Additionally, ''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:大甕神社]]'' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:日立市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines ''Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto''. Similarly, ''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:石井神社]]'' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:笠間市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south.
== Related Topics ==
* ''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]]'' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * ''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:天棚機姫神]]'' (also known as Amanoorihime)

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 倭文神社 |画像 = [[画像:Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|270px]]
倭文神社 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]西九条町二丁目14-2 |祭神 = [[天羽槌雄神|武羽槌雄神]]
[[経津主神]]
[[応神天皇|誉田別命]] |社格 = 旧村社{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=36}} |創建 = [[神護景雲]]2年([[768年]]) |本殿 = 三間社流造{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=912}} |例祭 = [[10月10日]] |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 39|緯度秒 = 24.51 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 48|経度秒 = 16.52 |地図 = Nara city }} '''倭文神社'''（しずりじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]西九条町二丁目にある神社。 ==歴史== [[神護景雲]]2年([[768年]])、[[武甕槌命]]が[[鹿島神宮]]より[[春日大社]]に遷座した際、供奉した[[中臣時風]]・[[中臣秀行|秀行]]がこの辰市の地に居住し、[[常陸国]]より祖神[[天羽槌雄神|武羽槌雄神]]（織部の神）を勧請したという{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}現地案内板{{sfn|皇室68号}}。[[倭文氏]]はその後裔で、辰市郷に住み神衣を織ったと伝わる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}現地案内板。古くから、「しずりのやしろ」あるいは「ひずりのやしろ」と呼ばれてきた{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}大和名所図会。 == 祭神 == * [[天羽槌雄神|武羽槌雄神]] * [[経津主神]] * [[応神天皇|誉田別命]] == 社殿 == === 本殿 === 三間社流造、向拝の柱間一間{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=912}}。 [[春日大社]]末社三十八所神社の旧本殿の移しと伝わる{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=912}}。 === 境内社 === ====若宮社==== 本殿の右に鎮座、祭神は[[仁徳天皇|大鷦鷯命]]{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。 ====蛇塚社==== 境内の東北隅に、独立したような形で鎮座する{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。祭神は[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]で、八阪神社とも呼ばれる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。桁行２尺、梁行１尺６寸の小さな社殿で、少し高くなった所に蛇塚がある{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。 == 祭事 == * 三月祈年祭 * 十月十日例祭{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}} * 十一月新嘗祭 * 毎月一日月次祭 === 蛇祭 === 例祭は'''蛇祭'''として知られる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。麦藁で5メートルほどの大蛇を造り、街を練り歩いたのち境内に納める{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。御供も蛇型で、サトイモの茎で作られる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。子供[[相撲]]が執り行われ、拝殿には[[1890年]]([[明治]]23年)の相撲の[[絵馬]]が掲げられている{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。 [[1915年]]([[大正]]4年)の神社調査書には、当時の祭礼の様子が次のように残されている{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。
「当社例祭ニハ十二ノ人形ヲ作リ饌御供ノ上ニ差シ込ミ別ニ大炬ヲ作リテ神社境内ニ持チ込ミ神饌ノ供進終ルヤ直チニ大炬ニ火ヲ放チ之ヲ焼キ盡スノ式アリ傳説ニ云フ往昔大蛇アリテ人ニ害ヲ為スコト往々アリ李(理)源大師深ク之レヲ憐ミ大蛇ヲ打チ平ケ人々ヲシテ安全ナラシメラレタリト云フ」{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}} == ギャラリー == File:Haiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|拝殿 File:Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|本殿 File:Wakiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|若宮社 File:Chozusha of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|手水舎 File:Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|蛇塚社 File:Honden of Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|蛇塚社 (社殿) File:Otabisho of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|御旅所 File:Otabisho Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|御旅所 (社殿) == 出典 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{commonscat|Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara)}} {{osm box|n|5262213988}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 5 神社|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 神社}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 8 建築|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 建築}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|title=奈良に残る「春日移し」のお社|date = 2015-秋|publisher = 皇室編集部|journal = 皇室|volume = 68|pages=87|ref={{sfnref|皇室68号}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しすりしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:春日移し|末]] {{神社 |名称 = 倭文神社 |画像 = [[画像:Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|270px]]
倭文神社 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]西九条町二丁目14-2 |祭神 = [[天羽槌雄神|武羽槌雄神]]
[[経津主神]]
[[応神天皇|誉田別命]] |社格 = 旧村社{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=36}} |創建 = [[神護景雲]]2年([[768年]]) |本殿 = 三間社流造{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=912}} |例祭 = [[10月10日]] |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 39|緯度秒 = 24.51 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 48|経度秒 = 16.52 |地図 = Nara city }} '''倭文神社'''（しずりじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]西九条町二丁目にある神社。 ==歴史== [[神護景雲]]2年([[768年]])、[[武甕槌命]]が[[鹿島神宮]]より[[春日大社]]に遷座した際、供奉した[[中臣時風]]・[[中臣秀行|秀行]]がこの辰市の地に居住し、[[常陸国]]より祖神[[天羽槌雄神|武羽槌雄神]]（織部の神）を勧請したという{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}現地案内板{{sfn|皇室68号}}。[[倭文氏]]はその後裔で、辰市郷に住み神衣を織ったと伝わる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}現地案内板。古くから、「しずりのやしろ」あるいは「ひずりのやしろ」と呼ばれてきた{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}大和名所図会。 == 祭神 == * [[天羽槌雄神|武羽槌雄神]] * [[経津主神]] * [[応神天皇|誉田別命]] == 社殿 == === 本殿 === 三間社流造、向拝の柱間一間{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=912}}。 [[春日大社]]末社三十八所神社の旧本殿の移しと伝わる{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=912}}。 === 境内社 === ====若宮社==== 本殿の右に鎮座、祭神は[[仁徳天皇|大鷦鷯命]]{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。 ====蛇塚社==== 境内の東北隅に、独立したような形で鎮座する{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。祭神は[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]で、八阪神社とも呼ばれる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。桁行２尺、梁行１尺６寸の小さな社殿で、少し高くなった所に蛇塚がある{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。 == 祭事 == * 三月祈年祭 * 十月十日例祭{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}} * 十一月新嘗祭 * 毎月一日月次祭 === 蛇祭 === 例祭は'''蛇祭'''として知られる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。麦藁で5メートルほどの大蛇を造り、街を練り歩いたのち境内に納める{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。御供も蛇型で、サトイモの茎で作られる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。子供[[相撲]]が執り行われ、拝殿には[[1890年]]([[明治]]23年)の相撲の[[絵馬]]が掲げられている{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。 [[1915年]]([[大正]]4年)の神社調査書には、当時の祭礼の様子が次のように残されている{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。
「当社例祭ニハ十二ノ人形ヲ作リ饌御供ノ上ニ差シ込ミ別ニ大炬ヲ作リテ神社境内ニ持チ込ミ神饌ノ供進終ルヤ直チニ大炬ニ火ヲ放チ之ヲ焼キ盡スノ式アリ傳説ニ云フ往昔大蛇アリテ人ニ害ヲ為スコト往々アリ李(理)源大師深ク之レヲ憐ミ大蛇ヲ打チ平ケ人々ヲシテ安全ナラシメラレタリト云フ」{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}} == ギャラリー == File:Haiden of Shizuri Shrine {{Shinto shrine |Name = '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社) |Image = [[File:Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|270px]]
{{ill|Shizuri Shrine|de|Shizuri-Schrein|ja|倭文神社|zh|Shizuri Shrine}} |Location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県|zh|奈良县}}{{ill|Nara (city)|de|Nara (Stadt)|ja|奈良市|zh|奈良市}} Nishikujō-chō 2-14-2 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|de|Futsunushi (German)|ja|経津主神|zh|Futsunushi (Chinese)}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|de|Kaiser Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|zh|Emperor Ōjin (Chinese)|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|lt_ja=誉田別命}} |Shrine rank = Former Village Shrine{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Shrines|p=36}} |Founded = {{ill|Jingo-keiun 2|de|Jingo-keiun 2 (German)|ja|神護景雲|zh|Jingo-keiun 2 (Chinese)}} ({{ill|768|de|768 (German)|ja|768年|zh|768 (Chinese)}}) |Main hall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}} |Annual festival = {{ill|October 10|de|10. Oktober|ja|10月10日|zh|10月10日}} |Cultural property = |ISO = JP-29 |lat deg = 34|lat min = 39|lat sec = 24.51 |lon deg =135|lon min = 48|lon sec = 16.52 |map = Nara city }} '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|Shintō-Schrein|ja|神社|zh|神社}} located in Nishikujō-chō 2-chōme of {{ill|Nara (city)|de|Nara (Stadt)|ja|奈良市|zh|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県|zh|奈良县}}. ==History== According to tradition, in {{ill|Jingo-keiun 2|de|Jingo-keiun 2 (German)|ja|神護景雲|zh|Jingo-keiun 2 (Chinese)}} ({{ill|768|de|768 (German)|ja|768年|zh|768 (Chinese)}}), when {{ill|Takemikazuchi|de|Takemikazuchi (German)|ja|武甕槌命|zh|Takemikazuchi (Chinese)}} was transferred from {{ill|Kashima Shrine|de|Kashima-Schrein|ja|鹿島神宮|zh|鹿島神宮}} to {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|de|Kasuga-Schrein|ja|春日大社|zh|春日大社}}, the accompanying {{ill|Nakatomi Tokikaze|de|Nakatomi Tokikaze (German)|ja|中臣時風|zh|Nakatomi Tokikaze (Chinese)}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Hideyuki|de|Nakatomi Hideyuki (German)|ja|中臣秀行|zh|Nakatomi Hideyuki (Chinese)|lt=Hideyuki|lt_ja=秀行}} settled in this Tatsuchi area. They enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神}} (the god of weaving), from {{ill|Hitachi Province|de|Provinz Hitachi|ja|常陸国|zh|常陸國}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board{{sfn|Imperial Household 68}} The {{ill|Shidori clan|de|Shidori-Clan|ja|倭文氏|zh|倭文氏}} are said to be their descendants, residing in Tatsuchi-gō and weaving sacred garments.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board Since ancient times, the shrine has been called either “Shizuri no Yashiro” or “Hizuri no Yashiro.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}''Yamato Meisho Zue'' ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|de|Futsunushi (German)|ja|経津主神|zh|Futsunushi (Chinese)}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|de|Kaiser Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|zh|Emperor Ōjin (Chinese)|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|lt_ja=誉田別命}} ==Shrine Buildings== ===Main Hall=== A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with a one-bay portico{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}}. It is said to be a relocated former main sanctuary of the Thirty-Eight Sub-Shrines of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|de|Kasuga-Schrein|ja|春日大社|zh|春日大社}}.{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}} ===Subsidiary Shrines=== ====Wakamiya Shrine==== Situated to the right of the main hall, enshrining {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|de|Kaiser Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|zh|仁德天皇|lt=Ōsazaki no Mikoto|lt_ja=大鷦鷯命}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ====Hebizuka Shrine==== Located in the northeastern corner of the grounds, standing somewhat separately.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} It enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|de|Susanoo (German)|ja|スサノオ|zh|須佐之男命}}, and is also called “Yasaka Shrine.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} The small sanctuary measures about 2 shaku in width by 1 shaku 6 sun in depth, and on a slightly raised area there is a “snake mound.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ==Festivals== * March: Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * October 10: Annual Festival{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} * November: Niinamesai (Harvest Festival) * First day of each month: Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival) ===Hebi Matsuri (Snake Festival)=== The annual festival is known as the '''Snake Festival''' (Hebi Matsuri).{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} A giant snake about five meters long is fashioned from straw, paraded through the streets, and then placed in the shrine grounds.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Offerings in the shape of snakes are also made, created from taro stalks.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Children’s {{ill|Sumo|de|Sumō|ja|相撲|zh|相撲}} matches are performed, and a sumo {{ill|ema (votive picture)|de|Ema (Votivbild)|ja|絵馬|zh|绘马}} from {{ill|1890|de|1890 (German)|ja|1890年|zh|1890 (Chinese)}} ({{ill|Meiji|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治|zh|明治}} 23) is displayed in the offering hall.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} According to a 1915 ({{ill|Taishō 4|de|Taishō 4 (German)|ja|大正|zh|大正}}) shrine survey, the festival at the time was recorded as follows:{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}}
“During the annual festival, twelve figures are crafted and inserted into the sacred offerings. A large torch (or straw snake) is also made and carried into the shrine precincts. As soon as the offering is completed, the torch is set aflame and burnt entirely. According to legend, in ancient times there was a giant snake that frequently harmed people. The great priest Ri (Li) Gen Daishi pitied the people’s plight, defeated the snake, and made the land safe.” ==Gallery== File:Haiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Haiden File:Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Main Hall File:Wakiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Wakamiya Shrine File:Chozusha of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Chōzuya Pavilion File:Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine File:Honden of Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine (sanctuary) File:Otabisho of Shizuri Shrine Oktober|ja|10月10日|zh|10月10日}} |Cultural property = |ISO = JP-29 |lat deg = 34|lat min = 39|lat sec = 24.51 |lon deg =135|lon min = 48|lon sec = 16.52 |map = Nara city }} '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|Shintō-Schrein|ja|神社|zh|神社}} located in Nishikujō-chō 2-chōme of {{ill|Nara (city)|de|Nara (Stadt)|ja|奈良市|zh|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県|zh|奈良县}}. ==History== According to tradition, in {{ill|Jingo-keiun 2|de|Jingo-keiun 2 (German)|ja|神護景雲|zh|Jingo-keiun 2 (Chinese)}} ({{ill|768|de|768 (German)|ja|768年|zh|768 (Chinese)}}), when {{ill|Takemikazuchi|de|Takemikazuchi (German)|ja|武甕槌命|zh|Takemikazuchi (Chinese)}} was transferred from {{ill|Kashima Shrine|de|Kashima-Schrein|ja|鹿島神宮|zh|鹿島神宮}} to {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|de|Kasuga-Schrein|ja|春日大社|zh|春日大社}}, the accompanying {{ill|Nakatomi Tokikaze|de|Nakatomi Tokikaze (German)|ja|中臣時風|zh|Nakatomi Tokikaze (Chinese)}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Hideyuki|de|Nakatomi Hideyuki (German)|ja|中臣秀行|zh|Nakatomi Hideyuki (Chinese)|lt=Hideyuki|lt_ja=秀行}} settled in this Tatsuchi area. They enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神}} (the god of weaving), from {{ill|Hitachi Province|de|Provinz Hitachi|ja|常陸国|zh|常陸國}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board{{sfn|Imperial Household 68}} The {{ill|Shidori clan|de|Shidori-Clan|ja|倭文氏|zh|倭文氏}} are said to be their descendants, residing in Tatsuchi-gō and weaving sacred garments.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board Since ancient times, the shrine has been called either “Shizuri no Yashiro” or “Hizuri no Yashiro.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}''Yamato Meisho Zue'' ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|de|Futsunushi (German)|ja|経津主神|zh|Futsunushi (Chinese)}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|de|Kaiser Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|zh|Emperor Ōjin (Chinese)|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|lt_ja=誉田別命}} ==Shrine Buildings== ===Main Hall=== A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with a one-bay portico{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}}. It is said to be a relocated former main sanctuary of the Thirty-Eight Sub-Shrines of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|de|Kasuga-Schrein|ja|春日大社|zh|春日大社}}.{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}} ===Subsidiary Shrines=== ====Wakamiya Shrine==== Situated to the right of the main hall, enshrining {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|de|Kaiser Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|zh|仁德天皇|lt=Ōsazaki no Mikoto|lt_ja=大鷦鷯命}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ====Hebizuka Shrine==== Located in the northeastern corner of the grounds, standing somewhat separately.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} It enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|de|Susanoo (German)|ja|スサノオ|zh|須佐之男命}}, and is also called “Yasaka Shrine.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} The small sanctuary measures about 2 shaku in width by 1 shaku 6 sun in depth, and on a slightly raised area there is a “snake mound.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ==Festivals== * March: Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * October 10: Annual Festival{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} * November: Niinamesai (Harvest Festival) * First day of each month: Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival) ===Hebi Matsuri (Snake Festival)=== The annual festival is known as the '''Snake Festival''' (Hebi Matsuri).{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} A giant snake about five meters long is fashioned from straw, paraded through the streets, and then placed in the shrine grounds.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Offerings in the shape of snakes are also made, created from taro stalks.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Children’s {{ill|Sumo|de|Sumō|ja|相撲|zh|相撲}} matches are performed, and a sumo {{ill|ema (votive picture)|de|Ema (Votivbild)|ja|絵馬|zh|绘马}} from {{ill|1890|de|1890 (German)|ja|1890年|zh|1890 (Chinese)}} ({{ill|Meiji|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治|zh|明治}} 23) is displayed in the offering hall.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} According to a 1915 ({{ill|Taishō 4|de|Taishō 4 (German)|ja|大正|zh|大正}}) shrine survey, the festival at the time was recorded as follows:{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}}
“During the annual festival, twelve figures are crafted and inserted into the sacred offerings. A large torch (or straw snake) is also made and carried into the shrine precincts. As soon as the offering is completed, the torch is set aflame and burnt entirely. According to legend, in ancient times there was a giant snake that frequently harmed people. The great priest Ri (Li) Gen Daishi pitied the people’s plight, defeated the snake, and made the land safe.” ==Gallery== File:Haiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Haiden File:Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Main Hall File:Wakiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Wakamiya Shrine File:Chozusha of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Chōzuya Pavilion File:Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine File:Honden of Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine (sanctuary) File:Otabisho of Shizuri Shrine Oktober|ja|10月10日|zh|10月10日}} |Cultural property = |ISO = JP-29 |lat deg = 34|lat min = 39|lat sec = 24.51 |lon deg =135|lon min = 48|lon sec = 16.52 |map = Nara city }} '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|Shintō-Schrein|ja|神社|zh|神社}} located in Nishikujō-chō 2-chōme of {{ill|Nara (city)|de|Nara (Stadt)|ja|奈良市|zh|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県|zh|奈良县}}. ==History== According to tradition, in {{ill|Jingo-keiun 2|de|Jingo-keiun 2 (German)|ja|神護景雲|zh|Jingo-keiun 2 (Chinese)}} ({{ill|768|de|768 (German)|ja|768年|zh|768 (Chinese)}}), when {{ill|Takemikazuchi|de|Takemikazuchi (German)|ja|武甕槌命|zh|Takemikazuchi (Chinese)}} was transferred from {{ill|Kashima Shrine|de|Kashima-Schrein|ja|鹿島神宮|zh|鹿島神宮}} to {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|de|Kasuga-Schrein|ja|春日大社|zh|春日大社}}, the accompanying {{ill|Nakatomi Tokikaze|de|Nakatomi Tokikaze (German)|ja|中臣時風|zh|Nakatomi Tokikaze (Chinese)}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Hideyuki|de|Nakatomi Hideyuki (German)|ja|中臣秀行|zh|Nakatomi Hideyuki (Chinese)|lt=Hideyuki|lt_ja=秀行}} settled in this Tatsuchi area. They enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神}} (the god of weaving), from {{ill|Hitachi Province|de|Provinz Hitachi|ja|常陸国|zh|常陸國}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board{{sfn|Imperial Household 68}} The {{ill|Shidori clan|de|Shidori-Clan|ja|倭文氏|zh|倭文氏}} are said to be their descendants, residing in Tatsuchi-gō and weaving sacred garments.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board Since ancient times, the shrine has been called either “Shizuri no Yashiro” or “Hizuri no Yashiro.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}''Yamato Meisho Zue'' ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|de|Futsunushi (German)|ja|経津主神|zh|Futsunushi (Chinese)}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|de|Kaiser Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|zh|Emperor Ōjin (Chinese)|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|lt_ja=誉田別命}} ==Shrine Buildings== ===Main Hall=== A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with a one-bay portico{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}}. It is said to be a relocated former main sanctuary of the Thirty-Eight Sub-Shrines of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|de|Kasuga-Schrein|ja|春日大社|zh|春日大社}}.{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}} ===Subsidiary Shrines=== ====Wakamiya Shrine==== Situated to the right of the main hall, enshrining {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|de|Kaiser Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|zh|仁德天皇|lt=Ōsazaki no Mikoto|lt_ja=大鷦鷯命}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ====Hebizuka Shrine==== Located in the northeastern corner of the grounds, standing somewhat separately.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} It enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|de|Susanoo (German)|ja|スサノオ|zh|須佐之男命}}, and is also called “Yasaka Shrine.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} The small sanctuary measures about 2 shaku in width by 1 shaku 6 sun in depth, and on a slightly raised area there is a “snake mound.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ==Festivals== * March: Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * October 10: Annual Festival{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} * November: Niinamesai (Harvest Festival) * First day of each month: Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival) ===Hebi Matsuri (Snake Festival)=== The annual festival is known as the '''Snake Festival''' (Hebi Matsuri).{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} A giant snake about five meters long is fashioned from straw, paraded through the streets, and then placed in the shrine grounds.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Offerings in the shape of snakes are also made, created from taro stalks.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Children’s {{ill|Sumo|de|Sumō|ja|相撲|zh|相撲}} matches are performed, and a sumo {{ill|ema (votive picture)|de|Ema (Votivbild)|ja|絵馬|zh|绘马}} from {{ill|1890|de|1890 (German)|ja|1890年|zh|1890 (Chinese)}} ({{ill|Meiji|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治|zh|明治}} 23) is displayed in the offering hall.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} According to a 1915 ({{ill|Taishō 4|de|Taishō 4 (German)|ja|大正|zh|大正}}) shrine survey, the festival at the time was recorded as follows:{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}}
“During the annual festival, twelve figures are crafted and inserted into the sacred offerings. A large torch (or straw snake) is also made and carried into the shrine precincts. As soon as the offering is completed, the torch is set aflame and burnt entirely. According to legend, in ancient times there was a giant snake that frequently harmed people. The great priest Ri (Li) Gen Daishi pitied the people’s plight, defeated the snake, and made the land safe.” ==Gallery== File:Haiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Haiden File:Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Main Hall File:Wakiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Wakamiya Shrine File:Chozusha of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Chōzuya Pavilion File:Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine File:Honden of Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine (sanctuary) File:Otabisho of Shizuri Shrine Oktober|ja|10月10日|zh|10月10日}} |Cultural property = |ISO = JP-29 |lat deg = 34|lat min = 39|lat sec = 24.51 |lon deg =135|lon min = 48|lon sec = 16.52 |map = Nara city }} '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|Shintō-Schrein|ja|神社|zh|神社}} located in Nishikujō-chō 2-chōme of {{ill|Nara (city)|de|Nara (Stadt)|ja|奈良市|zh|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県|zh|奈良县}}. ==History== According to tradition, in {{ill|Jingo-keiun 2|de|Jingo-keiun 2 (German)|ja|神護景雲|zh|Jingo-keiun 2 (Chinese)}} ({{ill|768|de|768 (German)|ja|768年|zh|768 (Chinese)}}), when {{ill|Takemikazuchi|de|Takemikazuchi (German)|ja|武甕槌命|zh|Takemikazuchi (Chinese)}} was transferred from {{ill|Kashima Shrine|de|Kashima-Schrein|ja|鹿島神宮|zh|鹿島神宮}} to {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|de|Kasuga-Schrein|ja|春日大社|zh|春日大社}}, the accompanying {{ill|Nakatomi Tokikaze|de|Nakatomi Tokikaze (German)|ja|中臣時風|zh|Nakatomi Tokikaze (Chinese)}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Hideyuki|de|Nakatomi Hideyuki (German)|ja|中臣秀行|zh|Nakatomi Hideyuki (Chinese)|lt=Hideyuki|lt_ja=秀行}} settled in this Tatsuchi area. They enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神}} (the god of weaving), from {{ill|Hitachi Province|de|Provinz Hitachi|ja|常陸国|zh|常陸國}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board{{sfn|Imperial Household 68}} The {{ill|Shidori clan|de|Shidori-Clan|ja|倭文氏|zh|倭文氏}} are said to be their descendants, residing in Tatsuchi-gō and weaving sacred garments.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board Since ancient times, the shrine has been called either “Shizuri no Yashiro” or “Hizuri no Yashiro.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}''Yamato Meisho Zue'' ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|de|Futsunushi (German)|ja|経津主神|zh|Futsunushi (Chinese)}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|de|Kaiser Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|zh|Emperor Ōjin (Chinese)|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|lt_ja=誉田別命}} ==Shrine Buildings== ===Main Hall=== A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with a one-bay portico{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}}. It is said to be a relocated former main sanctuary of the Thirty-Eight Sub-Shrines of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|de|Kasuga-Schrein|ja|春日大社|zh|春日大社}}.{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}} ===Subsidiary Shrines=== ====Wakamiya Shrine==== Situated to the right of the main hall, enshrining {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|de|Kaiser Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|zh|仁德天皇|lt=Ōsazaki no Mikoto|lt_ja=大鷦鷯命}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ====Hebizuka Shrine==== Located in the northeastern corner of the grounds, standing somewhat separately.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} It enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|de|Susanoo (German)|ja|スサノオ|zh|須佐之男命}}, and is also called “Yasaka Shrine.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} The small sanctuary measures about 2 shaku in width by 1 shaku 6 sun in depth, and on a slightly raised area there is a “snake mound.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ==Festivals== * March: Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * October 10: Annual Festival{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} * November: Niinamesai (Harvest Festival) * First day of each month: Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival) ===Hebi Matsuri (Snake Festival)=== The annual festival is known as the '''Snake Festival''' (Hebi Matsuri).{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} A giant snake about five meters long is fashioned from straw, paraded through the streets, and then placed in the shrine grounds.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Offerings in the shape of snakes are also made, created from taro stalks.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Children’s {{ill|Sumo|de|Sumō|ja|相撲|zh|相撲}} matches are performed, and a sumo {{ill|ema (votive picture)|de|Ema (Votivbild)|ja|絵馬|zh|绘马}} from {{ill|1890|de|1890 (German)|ja|1890年|zh|1890 (Chinese)}} ({{ill|Meiji|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治|zh|明治}} 23) is displayed in the offering hall.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} According to a 1915 ({{ill|Taishō 4|de|Taishō 4 (German)|ja|大正|zh|大正}}) shrine survey, the festival at the time was recorded as follows:{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}}
“During the annual festival, twelve figures are crafted and inserted into the sacred offerings. A large torch (or straw snake) is also made and carried into the shrine precincts. As soon as the offering is completed, the torch is set aflame and burnt entirely. According to legend, in ancient times there was a giant snake that frequently harmed people. The great priest Ri (Li) Gen Daishi pitied the people’s plight, defeated the snake, and made the land safe.” ==Gallery== File:Haiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Haiden File:Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Main Hall File:Wakiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Wakamiya Shrine File:Chozusha of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Chōzuya Pavilion File:Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine File:Honden of Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine (sanctuary) File:Otabisho of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Otabisho File:Otabisho Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Otabisho (sanctuary) ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Further Reading== {{commonscat|Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara)}} {{osm box|n|5262213988}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 5 神社|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History: Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 8 建築|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|Nara City History}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|title=奈良に残る「春日移し」のお社|date=2015-秋|publisher=皇室編集部|journal=皇室|volume=68|pages=87|ref={{sfnref|Imperial Household 68}}}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Translated pages]] {{draft categories| (あわじ市倭文委文)]] [[ja:南あわじ市]] [[ja:大甕神社]] [[ja:日立市]] [[ja:石井神社]] [[ja:笠間市]] [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] It is said to be a relocated former main sanctuary of the Thirty-Eight Sub-Shrines of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|de|Kasuga-Schrein|ja|春日大社|zh|春日大社}}.{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}} ===Subsidiary Shrines=== ====Wakamiya Shrine==== Situated to the right of the main hall, enshrining {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|de|Kaiser Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|zh|仁德天皇|lt=Ōsazaki no Mikoto|lt_ja=大鷦鷯命}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ====Hebizuka Shrine==== Located in the northeastern corner of the grounds, standing somewhat separately.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} It enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|de|Susanoo (German)|ja|スサノオ|zh|須佐之男命}}, and is also called “Yasaka Shrine.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} The small sanctuary measures about 2 shaku in width by 1 shaku 6 sun in depth, and on a slightly raised area there is a “snake mound.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ==Festivals== * March: Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * October 10: Annual Festival{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} * November: Niinamesai (Harvest Festival) * First day of each month: Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival) ===Hebi Matsuri (Snake Festival)=== The annual festival is known as the '''Snake Festival''' (Hebi Matsuri).{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} A giant snake about five meters long is fashioned from straw, paraded through the streets, and then placed in the shrine grounds.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Offerings in the shape of snakes are also made, created from taro stalks.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Children’s {{ill|Sumo|de|Sumō|ja|相撲|zh|相撲}} matches are performed, and a sumo {{ill|ema (votive picture)|de|Ema (Votivbild)|ja|絵馬|zh|绘马}} from {{ill|1890|de|1890 (German)|ja|1890年|zh|1890 (Chinese)}} ({{ill|Meiji|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治|zh|明治}} 23) is displayed in the offering hall.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} According to a 1915 ({{ill|Taishō 4|de|Taishō 4 (German)|ja|大正|zh|大正}}) shrine survey, the festival at the time was recorded as follows:{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}}
“During the annual festival, twelve figures are crafted and inserted into the sacred offerings. A large torch (or straw snake) is also made and carried into the shrine precincts. As soon as the offering is completed, the torch is set aflame and burnt entirely. According to legend, in ancient times there was a giant snake that frequently harmed people. The great priest Ri (Li) Gen Daishi pitied the people’s plight, defeated the snake, and made the land safe.” ==Gallery== File:Haiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Haiden File:Honden of Oktober|ja|10月10日|zh|10月10日}} |Cultural property = |ISO = JP-29 |lat deg = 34|lat min = 39|lat sec = 24.51 |lon deg =135|lon min = 48|lon sec = 16.52 |map = Nara city }} '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|Shintō-Schrein|ja|神社|zh|神社}} located in Nishikujō-chō 2-chōme of {{ill|Nara (city)|de|Nara (Stadt)|ja|奈良市|zh|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県|zh|奈良县}}. ==History== According to tradition, in {{ill|Jingo-keiun 2|de|Jingo-keiun 2 (German)|ja|神護景雲|zh|Jingo-keiun 2 (Chinese)}} ({{ill|768|de|768 (German)|ja|768年|zh|768 (Chinese)}}), when {{ill|Takemikazuchi|de|Takemikazuchi (German)|ja|武甕槌命|zh|Takemikazuchi (Chinese)}} was transferred from {{ill|Kashima Shrine|de|Kashima-Schrein|ja|鹿島神宮|zh|鹿島神宮}} to {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|de|Kasuga-Schrein|ja|春日大社|zh|春日大社}}, the accompanying {{ill|Nakatomi Tokikaze|de|Nakatomi Tokikaze (German)|ja|中臣時風|zh|Nakatomi Tokikaze (Chinese)}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Hideyuki|de|Nakatomi Hideyuki (German)|ja|中臣秀行|zh|Nakatomi Hideyuki (Chinese)|lt=Hideyuki|lt_ja=秀行}} settled in this Tatsuchi area. They enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神}} (the god of weaving), from {{ill|Hitachi Province|de|Provinz Hitachi|ja|常陸国|zh|常陸國}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board{{sfn|Imperial Household 68}} The {{ill|Shidori clan|de|Shidori-Clan|ja|倭文氏|zh|倭文氏}} are said to be their descendants, residing in Tatsuchi-gō and weaving sacred garments.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board Since ancient times, the shrine has been called either “Shizuri no Yashiro” or “Hizuri no Yashiro.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}''Yamato Meisho Zue'' ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|de|Futsunushi (German)|ja|経津主神|zh|Futsunushi (Chinese)}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|de|Kaiser Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|zh|Emperor Ōjin (Chinese)|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|lt_ja=誉田別命}} ==Shrine Buildings== ===Main Hall=== A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with a one-bay portico{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}}. It is said to be a relocated former main sanctuary of the Thirty-Eight Sub-Shrines of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|de|Kasuga-Schrein|ja|春日大社|zh|春日大社}}.{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}} ===Subsidiary Shrines=== ====Wakamiya Shrine==== Situated to the right of the main hall, enshrining {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|de|Kaiser Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|zh|仁德天皇|lt=Ōsazaki no Mikoto|lt_ja=大鷦鷯命}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ====Hebizuka Shrine==== Located in the northeastern corner of the grounds, standing somewhat separately.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} It enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|de|Susanoo (German)|ja|スサノオ|zh|須佐之男命}}, and is also called “Yasaka Shrine.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} The small sanctuary measures about 2 shaku in width by 1 shaku 6 sun in depth, and on a slightly raised area there is a “snake mound.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ==Festivals== * March: Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * October 10: Annual Festival{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} * November: Niinamesai (Harvest Festival) * First day of each month: Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival) ===Hebi Matsuri (Snake Festival)=== The annual festival is known as the '''Snake Festival''' (Hebi Matsuri).{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} A giant snake about five meters long is fashioned from straw, paraded through the streets, and then placed in the shrine grounds.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Offerings in the shape of snakes are also made, created from taro stalks.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Children’s {{ill|Sumo|de|Sumō|ja|相撲|zh|相撲}} matches are performed, and a sumo {{ill|ema (votive picture)|de|Ema (Votivbild)|ja|絵馬|zh|绘马}} from {{ill|1890|de|1890 (German)|ja|1890年|zh|1890 (Chinese)}} ({{ill|Meiji|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治|zh|明治}} 23) is displayed in the offering hall.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} According to a 1915 ({{ill|Taishō 4|de|Taishō 4 (German)|ja|大正|zh|大正}}) shrine survey, the festival at the time was recorded as follows:{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}}
“During the annual festival, twelve figures are crafted and inserted into the sacred offerings. A large torch (or straw snake) is also made and carried into the shrine precincts. As soon as the offering is completed, the torch is set aflame and burnt entirely. According to legend, in ancient times there was a giant snake that frequently harmed people. The great priest Ri (Li) Gen Daishi pitied the people’s plight, defeated the snake, and made the land safe.” ==Gallery== File:Haiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Haiden File:Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Main Hall File:Wakiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Wakamiya Shrine File:Chozusha of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Chōzuya Pavilion File:Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine File:Honden of Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine (sanctuary) File:Otabisho of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Otabisho File:Otabisho Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Otabisho (sanctuary) ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Further Reading== {{commonscat|Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara)}} {{osm box|n|5262213988}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 5 神社|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History: Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 8 建築|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|Nara City History}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|title=奈良に残る「春日移し」のお社|date=2015-秋|publisher=皇室編集部|journal=皇室|volume=68|pages=87|ref={{sfnref|Imperial Household 68}}}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Translated pages]] {{draft categories| {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|de|Kaiser Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|zh|Emperor Ōjin (Chinese)|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|lt_ja=誉田別命}} |Shrine rank = Former Village Shrine{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Shrines|p=36}} |Founded = {{ill|Jingo-keiun 2|de|Jingo-keiun 2 (German)|ja|神護景雲|zh|Jingo-keiun 2 (Chinese)}} ({{ill|768|de|768 (German)|ja|768年|zh|768 (Chinese)}}) |Main hall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}} |Annual festival = {{ill|October 10|de|10. Oktober|ja|10月10日|zh|10月10日}} |Cultural property = |ISO = JP-29 |lat deg = 34|lat min = 39|lat sec = 24.51 |lon deg =135|lon min = 48|lon sec = 16.52 |map = Nara city }} '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|Shintō-Schrein|ja|神社|zh|神社}} located in Nishikujō-chō 2-chōme of {{ill|Nara (city)|de|Nara (Stadt)|ja|奈良市|zh|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県|zh|奈良县}}. ==History== According to tradition, in {{ill|Jingo-keiun 2|de|Jingo-keiun 2 (German)|ja|神護景雲|zh|Jingo-keiun 2 (Chinese)}} ({{ill|768|de|768 (German)|ja|768年|zh|768 (Chinese)}}), when {{ill|Takemikazuchi|de|Takemikazuchi (German)|ja|武甕槌命|zh|Takemikazuchi (Chinese)}} was transferred from {{ill|Kashima Shrine|de|Kashima-Schrein|ja|鹿島神宮|zh|鹿島神宮}} to {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|de|Kasuga-Schrein|ja|春日大社|zh|春日大社}}, the accompanying {{ill|Nakatomi Tokikaze|de|Nakatomi Tokikaze (German)|ja|中臣時風|zh|Nakatomi Tokikaze (Chinese)}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Hideyuki|de|Nakatomi Hideyuki (German)|ja|中臣秀行|zh|Nakatomi Hideyuki (Chinese)|lt=Hideyuki|lt_ja=秀行}} settled in this Tatsuchi area. They enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神}} (the god of weaving), from {{ill|Hitachi Province|de|Provinz Hitachi|ja|常陸国|zh|常陸國}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board{{sfn|Imperial Household 68}} The {{ill|Shidori clan|de|Shidori-Clan|ja|倭文氏|zh|倭文氏}} are said to be their descendants, residing in Tatsuchi-gō and weaving sacred garments.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board Since ancient times, the shrine has been called either “Shizuri no Yashiro” or “Hizuri no Yashiro.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}''Yamato Meisho Zue'' ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|de|Futsunushi (German)|ja|経津主神|zh|Futsunushi (Chinese)}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|de|Kaiser Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|zh|Emperor Ōjin (Chinese)|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|lt_ja=誉田別命}} ==Shrine Buildings== ===Main Hall=== A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with a one-bay portico{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}}. It is said to be a relocated former main sanctuary of the Thirty-Eight Sub-Shrines of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|de|Kasuga-Schrein|ja|春日大社|zh|春日大社}}.{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}} ===Subsidiary Shrines=== ====Wakamiya Shrine==== Situated to the right of the main hall, enshrining {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|de|Kaiser Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|zh|仁德天皇|lt=Ōsazaki no Mikoto|lt_ja=大鷦鷯命}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ====Hebizuka Shrine==== Located in the northeastern corner of the grounds, standing somewhat separately.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} It enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|de|Susanoo (German)|ja|スサノオ|zh|須佐之男命}}, and is also called “Yasaka Shrine.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} The small sanctuary measures about 2 shaku in width by 1 shaku 6 sun in depth, and on a slightly raised area there is a “snake mound.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ==Festivals== * March: Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * October 10: Annual Festival{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} * November: Niinamesai (Harvest Festival) * First day of each month: Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival) ===Hebi Matsuri (Snake Festival)=== The annual festival is known as the '''Snake Festival''' (Hebi Matsuri).{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} A giant snake about five meters long is fashioned from straw, paraded through the streets, and then placed in the shrine grounds.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Offerings in the shape of snakes are also made, created from taro stalks.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Children’s {{ill|Sumo|de|Sumō|ja|相撲|zh|相撲}} matches are performed, and a sumo {{ill|ema (votive picture)|de|Ema (Votivbild)|ja|絵馬|zh|绘马}} from {{ill|1890|de|1890 (German)|ja|1890年|zh|1890 (Chinese)}} ({{ill|Meiji|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治|zh|明治}} 23) is displayed in the offering hall.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} According to a 1915 ({{ill|Taishō 4|de|Taishō 4 (German)|ja|大正|zh|大正}}) shrine survey, the festival at the time was recorded as follows:{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}}
“During the annual festival, twelve figures are crafted and inserted into the sacred offerings. A large torch (or straw snake) is also made and carried into the shrine precincts. As soon as the offering is completed, the torch is set aflame and burnt entirely. According to legend, in ancient times there was a giant snake that frequently harmed people. The great priest Ri (Li) Gen Daishi pitied the people’s plight, defeated the snake, and made the land safe.” ==Gallery== File:Haiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Haiden File:Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Main Hall File:Wakiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Wakamiya Shrine File:Chozusha of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Chōzuya Pavilion File:Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine File:Honden of Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine (sanctuary) File:Otabisho of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Otabisho File:Otabisho Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Otabisho (sanctuary) ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Further Reading== {{commonscat|Shizuri Shrine {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|de|Kaiser Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|zh|Emperor Ōjin (Chinese)|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|lt_ja=誉田別命}} |Shrine rank = Former Village Shrine{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Shrines|p=36}} |Founded = {{ill|Jingo-keiun 2|de|Jingo-keiun 2 (German)|ja|神護景雲|zh|Jingo-keiun 2 (Chinese)}} ({{ill|768|de|768 (German)|ja|768年|zh|768 (Chinese)}}) |Main hall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}} |Annual festival = {{ill|October 10|de|10. Oktober|ja|10月10日|zh|10月10日}} |Cultural property = |ISO = JP-29 |lat deg = 34|lat min = 39|lat sec = 24.51 |lon deg =135|lon min = 48|lon sec = 16.52 |map = Nara city }} '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|Shintō-Schrein|ja|神社|zh|神社}} located in Nishikujō-chō 2-chōme of {{ill|Nara (city)|de|Nara (Stadt)|ja|奈良市|zh|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県|zh|奈良县}}. ==History== According to tradition, in {{ill|Jingo-keiun 2|de|Jingo-keiun 2 (German)|ja|神護景雲|zh|Jingo-keiun 2 (Chinese)}} ({{ill|768|de|768 (German)|ja|768年|zh|768 (Chinese)}}), when {{ill|Takemikazuchi|de|Takemikazuchi (German)|ja|武甕槌命|zh|Takemikazuchi (Chinese)}} was transferred from {{ill|Kashima Shrine|de|Kashima-Schrein|ja|鹿島神宮|zh|鹿島神宮}} to {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|de|Kasuga-Schrein|ja|春日大社|zh|春日大社}}, the accompanying {{ill|Nakatomi Tokikaze|de|Nakatomi Tokikaze (German)|ja|中臣時風|zh|Nakatomi Tokikaze (Chinese)}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Hideyuki|de|Nakatomi Hideyuki (German)|ja|中臣秀行|zh|Nakatomi Hideyuki (Chinese)|lt=Hideyuki|lt_ja=秀行}} settled in this Tatsuchi area. They enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神}} (the god of weaving), from {{ill|Hitachi Province|de|Provinz Hitachi|ja|常陸国|zh|常陸國}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board{{sfn|Imperial Household 68}} The {{ill|Shidori clan|de|Shidori-Clan|ja|倭文氏|zh|倭文氏}} are said to be their descendants, residing in Tatsuchi-gō and weaving sacred garments.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board Since ancient times, the shrine has been called either “Shizuri no Yashiro” or “Hizuri no Yashiro.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}''Yamato Meisho Zue'' ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|de|Futsunushi (German)|ja|経津主神|zh|Futsunushi (Chinese)}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|de|Kaiser Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|zh|Emperor Ōjin (Chinese)|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|lt_ja=誉田別命}} ==Shrine Buildings== ===Main Hall=== A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with a one-bay portico{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}}. It is said to be a relocated former main sanctuary of the Thirty-Eight Sub-Shrines of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|de|Kasuga-Schrein|ja|春日大社|zh|春日大社}}.{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}} ===Subsidiary Shrines=== ====Wakamiya Shrine==== Situated to the right of the main hall, enshrining {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|de|Kaiser Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|zh|仁德天皇|lt=Ōsazaki no Mikoto|lt_ja=大鷦鷯命}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ====Hebizuka Shrine==== Located in the northeastern corner of the grounds, standing somewhat separately.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} It enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|de|Susanoo (German)|ja|スサノオ|zh|須佐之男命}}, and is also called “Yasaka Shrine.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} The small sanctuary measures about 2 shaku in width by 1 shaku 6 sun in depth, and on a slightly raised area there is a “snake mound.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ==Festivals== * March: Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * October 10: Annual Festival{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} * November: Niinamesai (Harvest Festival) * First day of each month: Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival) ===Hebi Matsuri (Snake Festival)=== The annual festival is known as the '''Snake Festival''' (Hebi Matsuri).{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} A giant snake about five meters long is fashioned from straw, paraded through the streets, and then placed in the shrine grounds.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Offerings in the shape of snakes are also made, created from taro stalks.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Children’s {{ill|Sumo|de|Sumō|ja|相撲|zh|相撲}} matches are performed, and a sumo {{ill|ema (votive picture)|de|Ema (Votivbild)|ja|絵馬|1=Ema (Shinto)|zh|绘马}} from {{ill|1890|de|1890 (German)|ja|1890年|zh|1890 (Chinese)}} ({{ill|Meiji|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治|zh|明治}} 23) is displayed in the offering hall.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} According to a 1915 ({{ill|Taishō 4|de|Taishō 4 (German)|ja|大正|zh|大正}}) shrine survey, the festival at the time was recorded as follows:{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}}
“During the annual festival, twelve figures are crafted and inserted into the sacred offerings. A large torch (or straw snake) is also made and carried into the shrine precincts. As soon as the offering is completed, the torch is set aflame and burnt entirely. According to legend, in ancient times there was a giant snake that frequently harmed people. The great priest Ri (Li) Gen Daishi pitied the people’s plight, defeated the snake, and made the land safe.” ==Gallery== File:Haiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Haiden File:Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Main Hall File:Wakiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Wakamiya Shrine File:Chozusha of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Chōzuya Pavilion File:Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine File:Honden of Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine (sanctuary) File:Otabisho of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Otabisho File:Otabisho Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Otabisho (sanctuary) ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Further Reading== {{commonscat|Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara)}} {{osm box|n|5262213988}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 5 神社|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History: Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 8 建築|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|Nara City History}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|title=奈良に残る「春日移し」のお社|date=2015-秋|publisher=皇室編集部|journal=皇室|volume=68|pages=87|ref={{sfnref|Imperial Household 68}}}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Translated pages]] {{draft categories| “During the annual festival, twelve figures are crafted and inserted into the sacred offerings. A large torch (or straw snake) is also made and carried into the shrine precincts. As soon as the offering is completed, the torch is set aflame and burnt entirely. According to legend, in ancient times there was a giant snake that frequently harmed people. The great priest Ri (Li) Gen Daishi pitied the people’s plight, defeated the snake, and made the land safe.” ==Gallery== File:Haiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Haiden File:Honden of {{ill|Futsunushi|de|Futsunushi (German)|ja|経津主神|zh|Futsunushi (Chinese)}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|de|Kaiser Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|zh|Emperor Ōjin (Chinese)|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|lt_ja=誉田別命}} |Shrine rank = Former Village Shrine{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Shrines|p=36}} |Founded = {{ill|Jingo-keiun 2|de|Jingo-keiun 2 (German)|ja|神護景雲|zh|Jingo-keiun 2 (Chinese)}} ({{ill|768|de|768 (German)|ja|768年|zh|768 (Chinese)}}) |Main hall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}} |Annual festival = {{ill|October 10|de|10. Oktober|ja|10月10日|zh|10月10日}} |Cultural property = |ISO = JP-29 |lat deg = 34|lat min = 39|lat sec = 24.51 |lon deg =135|lon min = 48|lon sec = 16.52 |map = Nara city }} '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|Shintō-Schrein|ja|神社|zh|神社}} located in Nishikujō-chō 2-chōme of {{ill|Nara (city)|de|Nara (Stadt)|ja|奈良市|zh|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県|zh|奈良县}}. ==History== According to tradition, in {{ill|Jingo-keiun 2|de|Jingo-keiun 2 (German)|ja|神護景雲|zh|Jingo-keiun 2 (Chinese)}} ({{ill|768|de|768 (German)|ja|768年|zh|768 (Chinese)}}), when {{ill|Takemikazuchi|de|Takemikazuchi (German)|ja|武甕槌命|zh|Takemikazuchi (Chinese)}} was transferred from {{ill|Kashima Shrine|de|Kashima-Schrein|ja|鹿島神宮|zh|鹿島神宮}} to {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|de|Kasuga-Schrein|ja|春日大社|zh|春日大社}}, the accompanying {{ill|Nakatomi Tokikaze|de|Nakatomi Tokikaze (German)|ja|中臣時風|zh|Nakatomi Tokikaze (Chinese)}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Hideyuki|de|Nakatomi Hideyuki (German)|ja|中臣秀行|zh|Nakatomi Hideyuki (Chinese)|lt=Hideyuki|lt_ja=秀行}} settled in this Tatsuchi area. They enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神}} (the god of weaving), from {{ill|Hitachi Province|de|Provinz Hitachi|ja|常陸国|zh|常陸國}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board{{sfn|Imperial Household 68}} The {{ill|Shizuri clan|de|Shidori-Clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|zh|倭文氏}} are said to be their descendants, residing in Tatsuchi-gō and weaving sacred garments.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board Since ancient times, the shrine has been called either “Shizuri no Yashiro” or “Hizuri no Yashiro.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}''Yamato Meisho Zue'' ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|de|Futsunushi (German)|ja|経津主神|zh|Futsunushi (Chinese)}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|de|Kaiser Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|zh|Emperor Ōjin (Chinese)|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|lt_ja=誉田別命}} ==Shrine Buildings== ===Main Hall=== A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with a one-bay portico{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}}. It is said to be a relocated former main sanctuary of the Thirty-Eight Sub-Shrines of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|de|Kasuga-Schrein|ja|春日大社|zh|春日大社}}.{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}} ===Subsidiary Shrines=== ====Wakamiya Shrine==== Situated to the right of the main hall, enshrining {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|de|Kaiser Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|zh|仁德天皇|lt=Ōsazaki no Mikoto|lt_ja=大鷦鷯命}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ====Hebizuka Shrine==== Located in the northeastern corner of the grounds, standing somewhat separately.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} It enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|de|Susanoo (German)|ja|スサノオ|zh|須佐之男命}}, and is also called “Yasaka Shrine.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} The small sanctuary measures about 2 shaku in width by 1 shaku 6 sun in depth, and on a slightly raised area there is a “snake mound.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ==Festivals== * March: Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * October 10: Annual Festival{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} * November: Niinamesai (Harvest Festival) * First day of each month: Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival) ===Hebi Matsuri (Snake Festival)=== The annual festival is known as the '''Snake Festival''' (Hebi Matsuri).{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} A giant snake about five meters long is fashioned from straw, paraded through the streets, and then placed in the shrine grounds.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Offerings in the shape of snakes are also made, created from taro stalks.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Children’s {{ill|Sumo|de|Sumō|ja|相撲|zh|相撲}} matches are performed, and a sumo {{ill|ema (votive picture)|de|Ema (Votivbild)|ja|絵馬|1=Ema (Shinto)|zh|绘马}} from {{ill|1890|de|1890 (German)|ja|1890年|zh|1890 (Chinese)}} ({{ill|Meiji|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治|zh|明治}} 23) is displayed in the offering hall.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} According to a 1915 ({{ill|Taishō 4|de|Taishō 4 (German)|ja|大正|zh|大正}}) shrine survey, the festival at the time was recorded as follows:{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}}
“During the annual festival, twelve figures are crafted and inserted into the sacred offerings. A large torch (or straw snake) is also made and carried into the shrine precincts. As soon as the offering is completed, the torch is set aflame and burnt entirely. According to legend, in ancient times there was a giant snake that frequently harmed people. The great priest Ri (Li) Gen Daishi pitied the people’s plight, defeated the snake, and made the land safe.” ==Gallery== File:Haiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Haiden File:Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Main Hall File:Wakiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Wakamiya Shrine File:Chozusha of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Chōzuya Pavilion File:Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine File:Honden of Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine (sanctuary) File:Otabisho of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Otabisho File:Otabisho Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Otabisho (sanctuary) ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Further Reading== {{commonscat|Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara)}} {{osm box|n|5262213988}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 5 神社|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History: Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 8 建築|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|Nara City History}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|title=奈良に残る「春日移し」のお社|date=2015-秋|publisher=皇室編集部|journal=皇室|volume=68|pages=87|ref={{sfnref|Imperial Household 68}}}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Translated pages]] {{draft categories| revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
Text replacement - "|明治|" to "|明治|1=Meiji era|"
3 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:倭文神社 (奈良市) up to rev 66509647
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 倭文神社 |画像 = [[画像:Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|270px]]
倭文神社 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]西九条町二丁目14-2 |祭神 = [[天羽槌雄神|武羽槌雄神]]
[[経津主神]]
[[応神天皇|誉田別命]] |社格 = 旧村社{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=36}} |創建 = [[神護景雲]]2年([[768年]]) |本殿 = 三間社流造{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=912}} |例祭 = [[10月10日]] |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 39|緯度秒 = 24.51 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 48|経度秒 = 16.52 |地図 = Nara city }} '''倭文神社'''（しずりじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]西九条町二丁目にある神社。 ==歴史== [[神護景雲]]2年([[768年]])、[[武甕槌命]]が[[鹿島神宮]]より[[春日大社]]に遷座した際、供奉した[[中臣時風]]・[[中臣秀行|秀行]]がこの辰市の地に居住し、[[常陸国]]より祖神[[天羽槌雄神|武羽槌雄神]]（織部の神）を勧請したという{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}現地案内板{{sfn|皇室68号}}。[[倭文氏]]はその後裔で、辰市郷に住み神衣を織ったと伝わる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}現地案内板。古くから、「しずりのやしろ」あるいは「ひずりのやしろ」と呼ばれてきた{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}大和名所図会。 == 祭神 == * [[天羽槌雄神|武羽槌雄神]] * [[経津主神]] * [[応神天皇|誉田別命]] == 社殿 == === 本殿 === 三間社流造、向拝の柱間一間{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=912}}。 [[春日大社]]末社三十八所神社の旧本殿の移しと伝わる{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=912}}。 === 境内社 === ====若宮社==== 本殿の右に鎮座、祭神は[[仁徳天皇|大鷦鷯命]]{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。 ====蛇塚社==== 境内の東北隅に、独立したような形で鎮座する{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。祭神は[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]で、八阪神社とも呼ばれる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。桁行２尺、梁行１尺６寸の小さな社殿で、少し高くなった所に蛇塚がある{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。 == 祭事 == * 三月祈年祭 * 十月十日例祭{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}} * 十一月新嘗祭 * 毎月一日月次祭 === 蛇祭 === 例祭は'''蛇祭'''として知られる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。麦藁で5メートルほどの大蛇を造り、街を練り歩いたのち境内に納める{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。御供も蛇型で、サトイモの茎で作られる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。子供[[相撲]]が執り行われ、拝殿には[[1890年]]([[明治]]23年)の相撲の[[絵馬]]が掲げられている{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。 [[1915年]]([[大正]]4年)の神社調査書には、当時の祭礼の様子が次のように残されている{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。
「当社例祭ニハ十二ノ人形ヲ作リ饌御供ノ上ニ差シ込ミ別ニ大炬ヲ作リテ神社境内ニ持チ込ミ神饌ノ供進終ルヤ直チニ大炬ニ火ヲ放チ之ヲ焼キ盡スノ式アリ傳説ニ云フ往昔大蛇アリテ人ニ害ヲ為スコト往々アリ李(理)源大師深ク之レヲ憐ミ大蛇ヲ打チ平ケ人々ヲシテ安全ナラシメラレタリト云フ」{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}} == ギャラリー == File:Haiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|拝殿 File:Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|本殿 File:Wakiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|若宮社 File:Chozusha of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|手水舎 File:Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|蛇塚社 File:Honden of Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|蛇塚社 (社殿) File:Otabisho of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|御旅所 File:Otabisho Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|御旅所 (社殿) == 出典 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{commonscat|Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara)}} {{osm box|n|5262213988}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 5 神社|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 神社}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 8 建築|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 建築}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|title=奈良に残る「春日移し」のお社|date = 2015-秋|publisher = 皇室編集部|journal = 皇室|volume = 68|pages=87|ref={{sfnref|皇室68号}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しすりしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:春日移し|末]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[倭文神社 (奈良市)]] to [[Shidori Shrine (Nara)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 倭文神社 |画像 = [[画像:Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|270px]]
倭文神社 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]西九条町二丁目14-2 |祭神 = [[天羽槌雄神|武羽槌雄神]]
[[経津主神]]
[[応神天皇|誉田別命]] |社格 = 旧村社{{sfn|奈良県史 神社|p=36}} |創建 = [[神護景雲]]2年([[768年]]) |本殿 = 三間社流造{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=912}} |例祭 = [[10月10日]] |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 39|緯度秒 = 24.51 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 48|経度秒 = 16.52 |地図 = Nara city }} '''倭文神社'''（しずりじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]西九条町二丁目にある神社。 ==歴史== [[神護景雲]]2年([[768年]])、[[武甕槌命]]が[[鹿島神宮]]より[[春日大社]]に遷座した際、供奉した[[中臣時風]]・[[中臣秀行|秀行]]がこの辰市の地に居住し、[[常陸国]]より祖神[[天羽槌雄神|武羽槌雄神]]（織部の神）を勧請したという{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}現地案内板{{sfn|皇室68号}}。[[倭文氏]]はその後裔で、辰市郷に住み神衣を織ったと伝わる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}現地案内板。古くから、「しずりのやしろ」あるいは「ひずりのやしろ」と呼ばれてきた{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}大和名所図会。 == 祭神 == * [[天羽槌雄神|武羽槌雄神]] * [[経津主神]] * [[応神天皇|誉田別命]] == 社殿 == === 本殿 === 三間社流造、向拝の柱間一間{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=912}}。 [[春日大社]]末社三十八所神社の旧本殿の移しと伝わる{{sfn|奈良県史 建築|p=912}}。 === 境内社 === ====若宮社==== 本殿の右に鎮座、祭神は[[仁徳天皇|大鷦鷯命]]{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。 ====蛇塚社==== 境内の東北隅に、独立したような形で鎮座する{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。祭神は[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]で、八阪神社とも呼ばれる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。桁行２尺、梁行１尺６寸の小さな社殿で、少し高くなった所に蛇塚がある{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。 == 祭事 == * 三月祈年祭 * 十月十日例祭{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}} * 十一月新嘗祭 * 毎月一日月次祭 === 蛇祭 === 例祭は'''蛇祭'''として知られる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=493}}。麦藁で5メートルほどの大蛇を造り、街を練り歩いたのち境内に納める{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。御供も蛇型で、サトイモの茎で作られる{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。子供[[相撲]]が執り行われ、拝殿には[[1890年]]([[明治]]23年)の相撲の[[絵馬]]が掲げられている{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。 [[1915年]]([[大正]]4年)の神社調査書には、当時の祭礼の様子が次のように残されている{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}}。
「当社例祭ニハ十二ノ人形ヲ作リ饌御供ノ上ニ差シ込ミ別ニ大炬ヲ作リテ神社境内ニ持チ込ミ神饌ノ供進終ルヤ直チニ大炬ニ火ヲ放チ之ヲ焼キ盡スノ式アリ傳説ニ云フ往昔大蛇アリテ人ニ害ヲ為スコト往々アリ李(理)源大師深ク之レヲ憐ミ大蛇ヲ打チ平ケ人々ヲシテ安全ナラシメラレタリト云フ」{{sfn|奈良市史|p=494}} == ギャラリー == File:Haiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|拝殿 File:Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|本殿 File:Wakiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|若宮社 File:Chozusha of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|手水舎 File:Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|蛇塚社 File:Honden of Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|蛇塚社 (社殿) File:Otabisho of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|御旅所 File:Otabisho Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|御旅所 (社殿) == 出典 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{commonscat|Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara)}} {{osm box|n|5262213988}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 5 神社|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 神社}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 8 建築|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史 建築}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|title=奈良に残る「春日移し」のお社|date = 2015-秋|publisher = 皇室編集部|journal = 皇室|volume = 68|pages=87|ref={{sfnref|皇室68号}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しすりしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:春日移し|末]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |Name = '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社) |image = [[File:Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|270px]]
{{ill|Shizuri Shrine|de|Shizuri-Schrein|ja|倭文神社|zh|Shizuri Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrine|qq=}} |Location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara (city)]] Nishikujō-chō 2-14-2 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}
[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]]
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Homuda Wake no Mikoto]] |Shrine Rank = Former Village Shrine{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Shrines|p=36}} |Founded = [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingo-keiun 2]] ([[:en:768|768]]) |Main hall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}} |Annual festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] |Cultural property = |ISO = JP-29 |lat deg = 34|lat min = 39|lat sec = 24.51 |lon deg =135|lon min = 48|lon sec = 16.52 |map = Nara city }} '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Nishikujō-chō 2-chōme of [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara (city)]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. ==History== According to tradition, in [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingo-keiun 2]] ([[:en:768|768]]), when {{ill|Takemikazuchi|de|Takemikazuchi (German)|ja|武甕槌命|zh|Takemikazuchi (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} was transferred from [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Shrine]] to [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Shrine]], the accompanying {{ill|Nakatomi Tokikaze|de|Nakatomi Tokikaze (German)|ja|中臣時風|zh|Nakatomi Tokikaze (Chinese)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Tokikaze|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Hideyuki|de|Nakatomi Hideyuki (German)|ja|中臣秀行|zh|Nakatomi Hideyuki (Chinese)|lt=Hideyuki|lt_ja=秀行|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Hideyuki|qq=}} settled in this Tatsuchi area. They enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (the god of weaving), from [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]].{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board{{sfn|Imperial Household 68}} The {{ill|Shizuri clan|de|Shidori-Clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|zh|倭文氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}} are said to be their descendants, residing in Tatsuchi-gō and weaving sacred garments.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board Since ancient times, the shrine has been called either “Shizuri no Yashiro” or “Hizuri no Yashiro.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}''Yamato Meisho Zue'' ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Homuda Wake no Mikoto]] ==Shrine Buildings== ===Main Hall=== A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with a one-bay portico{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}}. It is said to be a relocated former main sanctuary of the Thirty-Eight Sub-Shrines of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Shrine]].{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}} ===Subsidiary Shrines=== ====Wakamiya Shrine==== Situated to the right of the main hall, enshrining [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Ōsazaki no Mikoto]].{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ====Hebizuka Shrine==== Located in the northeastern corner of the grounds, standing somewhat separately.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} It enshrines [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and is also called “Yasaka Shrine.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} The small sanctuary measures about 2 shaku in width by 1 shaku 6 sun in depth, and on a slightly raised area there is a “snake mound.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ==Festivals== * March: Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * October 10: Annual Festival{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} * November: Niinamesai (Harvest Festival) * First day of each month: Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival) ===Hebi Matsuri (Snake Festival)=== The annual festival is known as the '''Snake Festival''' (Hebi Matsuri).{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} A giant snake about five meters long is fashioned from straw, paraded through the streets, and then placed in the shrine grounds.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Offerings in the shape of snakes are also made, created from taro stalks.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Children’s [[:en:Sumo|Sumo]] matches are performed, and a sumo [[:en:Ema (Shinto)|Ema (Shinto)]] from [[:en:1890|1890]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 23) is displayed in the offering hall.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} According to a 1915 ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō 4]]) shrine survey, the festival at the time was recorded as follows:{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}}
“During the annual festival, twelve figures are crafted and inserted into the sacred offerings. A large torch (or straw snake) is also made and carried into the shrine precincts. As soon as the offering is completed, the torch is set aflame and burnt entirely. According to legend, in ancient times there was a giant snake that frequently harmed people. The great priest Ri (Li) Gen Daishi pitied the people’s plight, defeated the snake, and made the land safe.” ==Gallery== File:Haiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Haiden File:Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Main Hall File:Wakiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Wakamiya Shrine File:Chozusha of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Chōzuya Pavilion File:Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine File:Honden of Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine (sanctuary) File:Otabisho of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Otabisho File:Otabisho Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Otabisho (sanctuary) ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Further Reading== {{commonscat|Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara)}} {{osm box|n|5262213988}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 5 神社|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History: Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 8 建築|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|Nara City History}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|title=奈良に残る「春日移し」のお社|date=2015-秋|publisher=皇室編集部|journal=皇室|volume=68|pages=87|ref={{sfnref|Imperial Household 68}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|version=66509647|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Weaving and textiles in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to textile deities]] [[Category:Mythological and legendary Japanese figures]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:recently translated Shizuri Faith]] }} [[sn:Shidori Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] [[ja:奈良市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (津山市油木北)]] [[ja:津山市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)]] [[ja:南あわじ市]] [[ja:大甕神社]] [[ja:日立市]] [[ja:石井神社]] [[ja:笠間市]] [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]]

(NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Otabisho File:Otabisho Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Otabisho (sanctuary) ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Further Reading== {{commonscat|Shizuri Shrine
