[[co:commons:Category:Shizuri-jinja (Saikujo-cho, Nara)]][[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Shizuri-jinja (Saikujo-cho, Nara)]][[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Shizuri-jinja (Saikujo-cho, Nara)]][[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] {{infobox religious building |Name = '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社) |image = File:Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg |Location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara (city)}} Nishikujō-chō 2-14-2 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto}} |Shrine Rank = Former Village Shrine{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Shrines|p=36}} |Founded = {{ill|Jingo-keiun|en|Jingo-keiun|WD=Q1373049|lt=Jingo-keiun 2}} ({{ill|768|en|768|WD=Q29961|lt=768}}) |Main hall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}} |Annual festival = {{ill|October 10|en|October 10|WD=Q2921|lt=October 10}} |Cultural property = |ISO = JP-29 |lat deg = 34|lat min = 39|lat sec = 24.51 |lon deg =135|lon min = 48|lon sec = 16.52 |map = Nara city |prefecture= {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.65680555555556|N|135.80458333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Amenohazuchi|WD=Q10940642}}; {{ill|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Nishikujō-chō 2-chōme of {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. ==History== According to tradition, in {{ill|Jingo-keiun|en|Jingo-keiun|WD=Q1373049|lt=Jingo-keiun 2}} ({{ill|768|en|768|WD=Q29961|lt=768}}), when {{ill|Takemikazuchi|de|Takemikazuchi (German)|ja|武甕槌命|zh|Takemikazuchi (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} was transferred from {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine}} to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga Shrine}}, the accompanying {{ill|Nakatomi Tokikaze|de|Nakatomi Tokikaze (German)|ja|中臣時風|zh|Nakatomi Tokikaze (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Tokikaze|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491612}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Hideyuki|de|Nakatomi Hideyuki (German)|ja|中臣秀行|zh|Nakatomi Hideyuki (Chinese)|lt=Hideyuki|lt_ja=秀行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Hideyuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491614}} settled in this Tatsuchi area. They enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (the god of weaving), from {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board{{sfn|Imperial Household 68}} The {{ill|Shizuri clan|de|Shidori-Clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|zh|倭文氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}} are said to be their descendants, residing in Tatsuchi-gō and weaving sacred garments.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board Since ancient times, the shrine has been called either “Shizuri no Yashiro” or “Hizuri no Yashiro.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}''Yamato Meisho Zue'' ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto}} ==Shrine Buildings== ===Main Hall=== A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with a one-bay portico{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}}. It is said to be a relocated former main sanctuary of the Thirty-Eight Sub-Shrines of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga Shrine}}.{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}} ===Subsidiary Shrines=== ====Wakamiya Shrine==== Situated to the right of the main hall, enshrining {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Ōsazaki no Mikoto}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ====Hebizuka Shrine==== Located in the northeastern corner of the grounds, standing somewhat separately.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} It enshrines {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and is also called “Yasaka Shrine.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} The small sanctuary measures about 2 shaku in width by 1 shaku 6 sun in depth, and on a slightly raised area there is a “snake mound.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ==Festivals== * March: Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * October 10: Annual Festival{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} * November: Niinamesai (Harvest Festival) * First day of each month: Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival) ===Hebi Matsuri (Snake Festival)=== The annual festival is known as the '''Snake Festival''' (Hebi Matsuri).{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} A giant snake about five meters long is fashioned from straw, paraded through the streets, and then placed in the shrine grounds.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Offerings in the shape of snakes are also made, created from taro stalks.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Children’s {{ill|Sumo|en|Sumo|WD=Q40561|lt=Sumo}} matches are performed, and a sumo {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|WD=Q687288|lt=Ema (Shinto)}} from {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 23) is displayed in the offering hall.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} According to a 1915 ({{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō 4}}) shrine survey, the festival at the time was recorded as follows:{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}}
“During the annual festival, twelve figures are crafted and inserted into the sacred offerings. A large torch (or straw snake) is also made and carried into the shrine precincts. As soon as the offering is completed, the torch is set aflame and burnt entirely. According to legend, in ancient times there was a giant snake that frequently harmed people. The great priest Ri (Li) Gen Daishi pitied the people’s plight, defeated the snake, and made the land safe.” ==Gallery== File:Haiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Haiden File:Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Main Hall File:Wakiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Wakamiya Shrine File:Chozusha of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Chōzuya Pavilion File:Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine File:Honden of Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine (sanctuary) File:Otabisho of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Otabisho File:Otabisho Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Otabisho (sanctuary) ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Further Reading== {{commonscat|Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara)}} {{osm box|n|5262213988}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 5 神社|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History: Shrines}}}} * {{Cite 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|Nara City History}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|title=奈良に残る「春日移し」のお社|date=2015-秋|publisher=皇室編集部|journal=皇室|volume=68|pages=87|ref={{sfnref|Imperial Household 68}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|version=66509647|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] [[ja:奈良市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (津山市油木北)]] [[ja:津山市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)]] [[ja:南あわじ市]] [[ja:大甕神社]] [[ja:日立市]] [[ja:石井神社]] [[ja:笠間市]] [[co:commons:Category:Shizuri-jinja (Saikujo-cho, Nara)]][[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Shizuri-jinja (Saikujo-cho, Nara)]][[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Shizuri-jinja (Saikujo-cho, Nara)]][[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] {{infobox religious building |Name = '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社) |image = File:Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg |Location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara (city)|1=Nara (city)}} Nishikujō-chō 2-14-2 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|1=Futsunushi}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |Shrine Rank = Former Village Shrine{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Shrines|p=36}} |Founded = {{ill|Jingo-keiun|en|Jingo-keiun|WD=Q1373049|lt=Jingo-keiun 2|1=Jingo-keiun}} ({{ill|768|en|768|WD=Q29961|lt=768|1=768}}) |Main hall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}} |Annual festival = {{ill|October 10|en|October 10|WD=Q2921|lt=October 10|1=October 10}} |Cultural property = |ISO = JP-29 |lat deg = 34|lat min = 39|lat sec = 24.51 |lon deg =135|lon min = 48|lon sec = 16.52 |map = Nara city |prefecture= {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.65680555555556|N|135.80458333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Amenohazuchi|WD=Q10940642|1=Takehazuchi}}; {{ill|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Nishikujō-chō 2-chōme of {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara (city)|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. ==History== According to tradition, in {{ill|Jingo-keiun|en|Jingo-keiun|WD=Q1373049|lt=Jingo-keiun 2|1=Jingo-keiun}} ({{ill|768|en|768|WD=Q29961|lt=768|1=768}}), when {{ill|Takemikazuchi|de|Takemikazuchi (German)|ja|武甕槌命|zh|Takemikazuchi (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} was transferred from {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine|1=Kashima Jingū}} to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga Shrine|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, the accompanying {{ill|Nakatomi Tokikaze|de|Nakatomi Tokikaze (German)|ja|中臣時風|zh|Nakatomi Tokikaze (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Tokikaze|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491612|1=Q135491612}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Hideyuki|de|Nakatomi Hideyuki (German)|ja|中臣秀行|zh|Nakatomi Hideyuki (Chinese)|lt=Hideyuki|lt_ja=秀行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Hideyuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491614|1=Nakatomi Hidetsura}} settled in this Tatsuchi area. They enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (the god of weaving), from {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province|1=Hitachi Province}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board{{sfn|Imperial Household 68}} The {{ill|Shizuri clan|de|Shidori-Clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|zh|倭文氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}} are said to be their descendants, residing in Tatsuchi-gō and weaving sacred garments.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board Since ancient times, the shrine has been called either “Shizuri no Yashiro” or “Hizuri no Yashiro.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}''Yamato Meisho Zue'' ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|1=Futsunushi}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ==Shrine Buildings== ===Main Hall=== A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with a one-bay portico{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}}. It is said to be a relocated former main sanctuary of the Thirty-Eight Sub-Shrines of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga Shrine|1=Kasuga-taisha}}.{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}} ===Subsidiary Shrines=== ====Wakamiya Shrine==== Situated to the right of the main hall, enshrining {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Ōsazaki no Mikoto|1=Emperor Nintoku}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ====Hebizuka Shrine==== Located in the northeastern corner of the grounds, standing somewhat separately.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} It enshrines {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and is also called “Yasaka Shrine.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} The small sanctuary measures about 2 shaku in width by 1 shaku 6 sun in depth, and on a slightly raised area there is a “snake mound.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ==Festivals== * March: Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * October 10: Annual Festival{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} * November: Niinamesai (Harvest Festival) * First day of each month: Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival) ===Hebi Matsuri (Snake Festival)=== The annual festival is known as the '''Snake Festival''' (Hebi Matsuri).{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} A giant snake about five meters long is fashioned from straw, paraded through the streets, and then placed in the shrine grounds.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Offerings in the shape of snakes are also made, created from taro stalks.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Children’s {{ill|Sumo|en|Sumo|WD=Q40561|lt=Sumo|1=Sumo}} matches are performed, and a sumo {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|WD=Q687288|lt=Ema (Shinto)|1=Ema (Shinto)}} from {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 23) is displayed in the offering hall.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} According to a 1915 ({{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō 4|1=Taishō era}}) shrine survey, the festival at the time was recorded as follows:{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}}
“During the annual festival, twelve figures are crafted and inserted into the sacred offerings. A large torch (or straw snake) is also made and carried into the shrine precincts. As soon as the offering is completed, the torch is set aflame and burnt entirely. According to legend, in ancient times there was a giant snake that frequently harmed people. The great priest Ri (Li) Gen Daishi pitied the people’s plight, defeated the snake, and made the land safe.” ==Gallery== File:Haiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Haiden File:Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Main Hall File:Wakiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Wakamiya Shrine File:Chozusha of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Chōzuya Pavilion File:Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine File:Honden of Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine (sanctuary) File:Otabisho of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Otabisho File:Otabisho Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Otabisho (sanctuary) ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Further Reading== {{commonscat|Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara)}} {{osm box|n|5262213988}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 5 神社|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1989|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History: Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 8 建築|editor=奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=名著出版|year=1998|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|Nara City History}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|title=奈良に残る「春日移し」のお社|date=2015-秋|publisher=皇室編集部|journal=皇室|volume=68|pages=87|ref={{sfnref|Imperial Household 68}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|version=66509647|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] [[ja:奈良市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (津山市油木北)]] [[ja:津山市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)]] [[ja:南あわじ市]] [[ja:大甕神社]] [[ja:日立市]] [[ja:石井神社]] [[ja:笠間市]] enshrines {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and is also called “Yasaka Shrine.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} The small sanctuary {{ill|Sumo|en|Sumo|WD=Q40561|lt=Sumo|1=Sumo}} matches are performed, and a sumo {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|WD=Q687288|lt=Ema (Shinto)|1=Ema (Shinto)}} from {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 23) is displayed in the offering hall.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} According to a 1915 ({{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō 4|1=Taishō era}}) shrine survey, the festival at the time was recorded as follows:{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}}

[[co:commons:Category:Shizuri-jinja (Saikujo-cho, Nara)]][[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Shizuri-jinja (Saikujo-cho, Nara)]][[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Shizuri-jinja (Saikujo-cho, Nara)]][[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] {{infobox religious building |Name = '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社) |image = File:Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg |Location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara (city)|1=Nara (city)}} Nishikujō-chō 2-14-2 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|1=Futsunushi}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |Shrine Rank = Former Village Shrine{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Shrines|p=36}} |Founded = {{ill|Jingo-keiun|en|Jingo-keiun|WD=Q1373049|lt=Jingo-keiun 2|1=Jingo-keiun}} ({{ill|768|en|768|WD=Q29961|lt=768|1=768}}) |Main hall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}} |Annual festival = {{ill|October 10|en|October 10|WD=Q2921|lt=October 10|1=October 10}} |Cultural property = |ISO = JP-29 |lat deg = 34|lat min = 39|lat sec = 24.51 |lon deg =135|lon min = 48|lon sec = 16.52 |map = Nara city |prefecture= {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.65680555555556|N|135.80458333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Amenohazuchi|WD=Q10940642|1=Takehazuchi}}; {{ill|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Nishikujō-chō 2-chōme of {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara (city)|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. ==History== According to tradition, in {{ill|Jingo-keiun|en|Jingo-keiun|WD=Q1373049|lt=Jingo-keiun 2|1=Jingo-keiun}} ({{ill|768|en|768|WD=Q29961|lt=768|1=768}}), when {{ill|Takemikazuchi|de|Takemikazuchi (German)|ja|武甕槌命|zh|Takemikazuchi (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} was transferred from {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine|1=Kashima Jingū}} to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga Shrine|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, the accompanying {{ill|Nakatomi Tokikaze|de|Nakatomi Tokikaze (German)|ja|中臣時風|zh|Nakatomi Tokikaze (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Tokikaze|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491612|1=Q135491612}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Hideyuki|de|Nakatomi Hideyuki (German)|ja|中臣秀行|zh|Nakatomi Hideyuki (Chinese)|lt=Hideyuki|lt_ja=秀行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Hideyuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491614|1=Nakatomi Hidetsura}} settled in this Tatsuchi area. They enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (the god of weaving), from {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province|1=Hitachi Province}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board{{sfn|Imperial Household 68}} The {{ill|Shizuri clan|de|Shidori-Clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|zh|倭文氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}} are said to be their descendants, residing in Tatsuchi-gō and weaving sacred garments.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board Since ancient times, the shrine has been called either “Shizuri no Yashiro” or “Hizuri no Yashiro.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}''Yamato Meisho Zue'' ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|1=Futsunushi}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ==Shrine Buildings== ===Main Hall=== A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with a one-bay portico{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}}. It is said to be a relocated former main sanctuary of the Thirty-Eight Sub-Shrines of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga Shrine|1=Kasuga-taisha}}.{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}} ===Subsidiary Shrines=== ====Wakamiya Shrine==== Situated to the right of the main hall, enshrining {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Ōsazaki no Mikoto|1=Emperor Nintoku}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ====Hebizuka Shrine==== Located in the northeastern corner of the grounds, standing somewhat separately.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} It enshrines {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and is also called “Yasaka Shrine.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} The small sanctuary measures about 2 shaku in width by 1 shaku 6 sun in depth, and on a slightly raised area there is a “snake mound.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ==Festivals== * March: Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * October 10: Annual Festival{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} * November: Niinamesai (Harvest Festival) * First day of each month: Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival) ===Hebi Matsuri (Snake Festival)=== The annual festival is known as the '''Snake Festival''' (Hebi Matsuri).{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} A giant snake about five meters long is fashioned from straw, paraded through the streets, and then placed in the shrine grounds.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Offerings in the shape of snakes are also made, created from taro stalks.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Children’s {{ill|Sumo|en|Sumo|WD=Q40561|lt=Sumo|1=Sumo}} matches are performed, and a sumo {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|WD=Q687288|lt=Ema (Shinto)|1=Ema (Shinto)}} from {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 23) is displayed in the offering hall.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} According to a 1915 ({{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō 4|1=Taishō era}}) shrine survey, the festival at the time was recorded as follows:{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}}
“During the annual festival, twelve figures are crafted and inserted into the sacred offerings. A large torch (or straw snake) is also made and carried into the shrine precincts. As soon as the offering is completed, the torch is set aflame and burnt entirely. According to legend, in ancient times there was a giant snake that frequently harmed people. The great priest Ri (Li) Gen Daishi pitied the people’s plight, defeated the snake, and made the land safe.” ==Gallery== File:Haiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Haiden File:Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Main Hall File:Wakiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Wakamiya Shrine File:Chozusha of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Chōzuya Pavilion File:Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine File:Honden of Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine (sanctuary) File:Otabisho of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Otabisho File:Otabisho Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Otabisho (sanctuary) ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Further Reading== {{commonscat|Shizuri Shrine revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Shizuri-jinja (Saikujo-cho, Nara)]][[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Shizuri-jinja (Saikujo-cho, Nara)]][[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] [[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] {{infobox religious building |Name = '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社) |image = File:Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg |Location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara (city)|1=Nara (city)}} Nishikujō-chō 2-14-2 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|1=Futsunushi}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |Shrine Rank = Former Village Shrine{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Shrines|p=36}} |Founded = {{ill|Jingo-keiun|en|Jingo-keiun|WD=Q1373049|lt=Jingo-keiun 2|1=Jingo-keiun}} ({{ill|768|en|768|WD=Q29961|lt=768|1=768}}) |Main hall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}} |Annual festival = {{ill|October 10|en|October 10|WD=Q2921|lt=October 10|1=October 10}} |Cultural property = |ISO = JP-29 |lat deg = 34|lat min = 39|lat sec = 24.51 |lon deg =135|lon min = 48|lon sec = 16.52 |map = Nara city |prefecture= {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.65680555555556|N|135.80458333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Amenohazuchi|WD=Q10940642|1=Takehazuchi}}; {{ill|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} '''Shizuri Shrine''' (倭文神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Nishikujō-chō 2-chōme of {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara (city)|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. ==History== According to tradition, in {{ill|Jingo-keiun|en|Jingo-keiun|WD=Q1373049|lt=Jingo-keiun 2|1=Jingo-keiun}} ({{ill|768|en|768|WD=Q29961|lt=768|1=768}}), when {{ill|Takemikazuchi|de|Takemikazuchi (German)|ja|武甕槌命|zh|Takemikazuchi (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} was transferred from {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine|1=Kashima Jingū}} to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga Shrine|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, the accompanying {{ill|Nakatomi Tokikaze|de|Nakatomi Tokikaze (German)|ja|中臣時風|zh|Nakatomi Tokikaze (Chinese)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Tokikaze|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491612|1=Q135491612}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Hideyuki|de|Nakatomi Hideyuki (German)|ja|中臣秀行|zh|Nakatomi Hideyuki (Chinese)|lt=Hideyuki|lt_ja=秀行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Hideyuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491614|1=Nakatomi Hidetsura}} settled in this Tatsuchi area. They enshrined their ancestral deity, {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (the god of weaving), from {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province|1=Hitachi Province}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board{{sfn|Imperial Household 68}} The {{ill|Shizuri clan|de|Shidori-Clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|zh|倭文氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}} are said to be their descendants, residing in Tatsuchi-gō and weaving sacred garments.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}On-site information board Since ancient times, the shrine has been called either “Shizuri no Yashiro” or “Hizuri no Yashiro.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}}''Yamato Meisho Zue'' ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Takehazuchi|de|Takehazuchi (German)|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|zh|Takehazuchi (Chinese)|lt=Takehazuchi|lt_ja=武羽槌雄神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|1=Futsunushi}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Wake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ==Shrine Buildings== ===Main Hall=== A three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with a one-bay portico{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}}. It is said to be a relocated former main sanctuary of the Thirty-Eight Sub-Shrines of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga Shrine|1=Kasuga-taisha}}.{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History: Architecture|p=912}} ===Subsidiary Shrines=== ====Wakamiya Shrine==== Situated to the right of the main hall, enshrining {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Ōsazaki no Mikoto|1=Emperor Nintoku}}.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ====Hebizuka Shrine==== Located in the northeastern corner of the grounds, standing somewhat separately.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} It enshrines {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and is also called “Yasaka Shrine.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} The small sanctuary measures about 2 shaku in width by 1 shaku 6 sun in depth, and on a slightly raised area there is a “snake mound.”{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} ==Festivals== * March: Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * October 10: Annual Festival{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} * November: Niinamesai (Harvest Festival) * First day of each month: Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival) ===Hebi Matsuri (Snake Festival)=== The annual festival is known as the '''Snake Festival''' (Hebi Matsuri).{{sfn|Nara City History|p=493}} A giant snake about five meters long is fashioned from straw, paraded through the streets, and then placed in the shrine grounds.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Offerings in the shape of snakes are also made, created from taro stalks.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} Children’s {{ill|Sumo|en|Sumo|WD=Q40561|lt=Sumo|1=Sumo}} matches are performed, and a sumo {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|WD=Q687288|lt=Ema (Shinto)|1=Ema (Shinto)}} from {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 23) is displayed in the offering hall.{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}} According to a 1915 ({{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō 4|1=Taishō era}}) shrine survey, the festival at the time was recorded as follows:{{sfn|Nara City History|p=494}}
“During the annual festival, twelve figures are crafted and inserted into the sacred offerings. A large torch (or straw snake) is also made and carried into the shrine precincts. As soon as the offering is completed, the torch is set aflame and burnt entirely. According to legend, in ancient times there was a giant snake that frequently harmed people. The great priest Ri (Li) Gen Daishi pitied the people’s plight, defeated the snake, and made the land safe.” ==Gallery== File:Haiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Haiden File:Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Main Hall File:Wakiden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Wakamiya Shrine File:Chozusha of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Chōzuya Pavilion File:Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine File:Honden of Hebizuka Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Hebizuka Shrine (sanctuary) File:Otabisho of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Otabisho File:Otabisho Honden of Shizuri Shrine (NishiKujo-cho 2, Nara).jpg|Otabisho (sanctuary) ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Further Reading== {{commonscat|Shizuri Shrine 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|Nara City History}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|title=奈良に残る「春日移し」のお社|date=2015-秋|publisher=皇室編集部|journal=皇室|volume=68|pages=87|ref={{sfnref|Imperial Household 68}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated page|ja|倭文神社
